name: Phase 4: WHIR
summary:
The WHIR phase is the final leg of the recursive verifier. Phase 3 (Stacking) handed us an initial whir_claim, the Mobius-kernel random coefficient mu, and the opening point u_cube. verify_whir must now reduce that claim down to a Reed–Solomon codeword equality and discharge it through num_whir_rounds rounds of folding sumcheck, codeword commitments, out-of-domain (OOD) sampling, proof-of-work, Merkle-verified openings, binary k-folds, and a final accumulated claim check.

Eight AIRs split the work: WhirRoundAir is the per-round coordinator and the point of contact with Stacking; SumcheckAir runs the inner folding sumcheck; WhirQueryAir generates and dispatches queries; InitialOpenedValuesAir and NonInitialOpenedValuesAir verify opened rows against Merkle roots; WhirFoldingAir contracts 2k opened values down to one yi; and FinalPolyMleEvalAir plus FinalPolyQueryEvalAir compute the MLE prefix·suffix and query-evaluation terms used in the final equality check.

blocks.html: The order below follows the verifier's control flow: WhirRoundAir first (it dispatches everything else), then the inner SumcheckAir, then the query-phase AIRs (WhirQueryAir, InitialOpenedValuesAir, NonInitialOpenedValuesAir, WhirFoldingAir), and finally the closing AIRs (FinalPolyMleEvalAir, FinalPolyQueryEvalAir) that realize the acc == claim check.

blocks.heading: WhirRoundAir (per-round coordinator)
blocks.html: WhirRoundAir has one row per proof per WHIR round. It receives the starting claim from Stacking via WhirModuleBus, drives the sumcheck via WhirSumcheckBus, publishes the codeword commitment on CommitmentsBus, samples OOD / query-PoW / gamma on the transcript, and dispatches per-query work to WhirQueryAir via VerifyQueriesBus.

blocks.heading: WhirRoundCols: per-round state
blocks.code:
#[repr(C)]
#[derive(AlignedBorrow)]
pub struct WhirRoundCols<T, const ENC_WIDTH: usize> {
    pub is_enabled: T,
    pub proof_idx: T,
    pub whir_round: T,
    pub is_first_in_proof: T,
    pub tidx: T,
    pub num_queries: T,
    pub omega: T,
    pub z0: [T; D_EF],
    pub y0: [T; D_EF],
    pub commit: [T; DIGEST_SIZE],
    pub final_poly_mle_eval: [T; D_EF],
    pub query_pow_witness: T,
    pub query_pow_sample: T,
    pub gamma: [T; D_EF],
    pub claim: [T; D_EF],
    pub next_claim: [T; D_EF],
    pub post_sumcheck_claim: [T; D_EF],
    pub whir_round_enc: [T; ENC_WIDTH],
}
blocks.explain: Every round row carries: the starting claim from the previous round (or from Stacking for whir_round = 0), the sumcheck output post_sumcheck_claim, the codeword commit, the OOD point/value z0 / y0, a per-round query_pow_witness for the query-phase PoW, the sampled gamma, and next_claim which is the claim passed to the next round. The whir_round_enc encodes the round index in log-space so that constraints like num_queries_per_round[round] can be table-looked-up without huge selector degree.

blocks.heading: Encoding the round index and per-round num_queries
blocks.explain: The Encoder turns the one-hot round selector into a low-degree expression. whir_round is constrained to equal the decoded index, and num_queries is constrained to the VK-fixed table num_queries_per_round[round]. These two facts let every downstream bus message (CommitmentsBus, VerifyQueriesBus, …) carry the correct round-dependent values without a dynamic lookup.

blocks.heading: omega initialization and per-round squaring
blocks.explain: omega is the generator of the RS evaluation domain. On the first row of each proof it is pinned to the initial_log_domain_size-th root of unity; on every intra-proof transition it is squared, mirroring the domain halving that WHIR performs between rounds. Subsequent AIRs (WhirQueryAir) read this column to compute zi_root = omega^index.

blocks.heading: Round counter structure (NestedForLoopSubAir)
blocks.explain: A two-level nested loop (proof, round) forces contiguous num_rounds rows per proof, and the penultimate constraint pins the last row's whir_round to num_rounds - 1. Finally, next.claim = local.next_claim chains the running claim from one round to the next — so whatever round i declares as its output is what round i+1 must accept as its input.

blocks.heading: Receive the starting claim from Stacking; publish codeword commit
blocks.explain: WhirModuleBus.receive at is_proof_start pulls (tidx_whir, whir_claim) from Stacking — so round 0 begins with the exact handoff produced in Phase 3. CommitmentsBus.add_key_with_lookups publishes the codeword commitment under (major_idx = round+1, minor_idx = 0) with a multiplicity of next.num_queries: that's how many times NonInitialOpenedValuesAir will Merkle-verify against this root on the next round. The gate is_same_proof skips the final round (there is no commitment after the last fold).

blocks.heading: Dispatch sumcheck and observe codeword_commit / z0 / y0
blocks.explain: sumcheck_bus.send dispatches exactly one group of k_whir SumcheckAir rows with sumcheck_idx = round * k; the sumcheck's input claim is local.claim and its output claim is local.post_sumcheck_claim. The transcript block (gated by is_same_proof — i.e. non-final rounds) performs the three classic WHIR round actions: (a) observe the codeword commitment, (b) sample the OOD point z0, (c) observe the OOD evaluation y0. The tidx offsets (3*D_EF + folding_pow_offset) * k) are exactly what the prover will feed on TranscriptBus.

blocks.heading: Broadcast z0 as query 0 and dispatch final-poly checks
blocks.explain: Query 0 is reserved for the OOD point: WhirQueryBusMessage { query_idx: 0, value: z0 } seeds FinalPolyQueryEvalAir's Horner evaluation at z0. On the final round (is_enabled - is_same_proof, i.e. last row of each proof) two more buses fire: FinalPolyMleEvalBus consumes eval_mle(final_poly, u[t..]) and FinalPolyQueryEvalBus consumes next_claim - final_poly_mle_eval. Their receivers (the two final-poly AIRs) will force those values to match the query-side accumulator, closing the M-WHIR extraction argument.

blocks.heading: Per-round query-phase PoW
blocks.explain: Every round (including the final one, via final_round_offset) carries a query-phase PoW. The witness is observed, a sample is drawn, and then ExpBitsLenBus verifies generator^{sample[0..pow_bits]} == 1, i.e. the top pow_bits of sample are zero. Together with the transcript permutation, this makes biasing the query sample computationally expensive.

blocks.heading: Dispatch per-query verification, sample gamma, accumulate claim
blocks.explain: This block is the heart of the per-round claim accumulation (whir-10). It dispatches num_queries rows to WhirQueryAir via VerifyQueriesBus, pre-loading pre_claim = post_sumcheck_claim + gamma * y0 (so that the per-query yi contributions can be folded in with a Horner step using gamma) and asserting post_claim = next_claim, which will be chained back in next.claim. Then gamma itself is sampled on the transcript and published on WhirGammaBus for FinalPolyQueryEvalAir to consume. The next.tidx assertion advances the transcript cursor across num_queries sampled bits plus the gamma extension-field sample.

blocks.html: That completes the WhirRoundAir row. Let's zoom into the inner sumcheck it dispatches.

blocks.heading: SumcheckAir (inner folding sumcheck)
blocks.html: SumcheckAir has k_whir * num_whir_rounds rows per proof. Each row is one sub-round: observe (ev1, ev2), optionally run a folding PoW, sample alpha, and interpolate claim = eval_quadratic([claim - ev1, ev1, ev2], alpha). Rows are grouped into chunks of k_whir that together form one WHIR round's sumcheck.

blocks.heading: SumcheckCols
blocks.explain: subidx cycles 0..k_whir-1 within each round. pre_claim is the claim at the start of the sub-round, post_group_claim is the claim at the end of the entire k_whir-block (and is the same on every row of that group). eq_partial accumulates ∏j mobius_eq_1(u[j], alpha[j]) across sub-rounds. u is the opening point coordinate for this sub-round, pulled from WhirOpeningPointBus (the permutation bus that Stacking produces).

blocks.heading: Row window and sub-round NestedForLoopSubAir
blocks.explain: A three-level nested loop (proof, round, subidx) enforces contiguous rows and exactly k_whir sub-rounds per round. The last row of each round must have subidx = k_whir - 1, so every sumcheck group is complete.

blocks.heading: Receive claim, interpolate, chain to next sub-round
blocks.explain: On is_first_in_round the WhirSumcheckBus is received: this discharges the dispatch that WhirRoundAir sent. Then post_claim = interpolate([pre_claim - ev1, ev1, ev2], alpha) is computed (via interpolate_quadratic) and chained: on intra-round transitions next.pre_claim = post_claim, and on the last row of the group post_group_claim = post_claim. The post_group_claim is the same across every row of the group, mirroring the bus contract with WhirRoundAir. The tidx stride is 3*D_EF + folding_pow_offset per sub-round.

blocks.heading: Accumulate eq_partial across sub-rounds
blocks.explain: eq_partial is the running product ∏j mobius_eq_1(u[j], alpha[j]). It starts at mobius_eq_1(u[0], alpha[0]) on the first row of each proof and multiplies by a fresh factor on every transition. Its final value (on the last sumcheck row of the proof) will be pulled by FinalPolyMleEvalAir via WhirEqAlphaUBus and multiplied by the MLE suffix to form the “prefix · suffix” part of the claim.

blocks.heading: Observe (ev1, ev2) and sample alpha on the transcript
blocks.explain: This is the scene that whir-1 visualises: the per-sub-round transcript moves. ev1 and ev2 (the quadratic-sumcheck messages) are observed, then, if folding_pow_bits > 0, a folding PoW witness and sample are observed/sampled and checked via ExpBitsLenBus (the same LT-correctness argument used in Phase 2). Finally, alpha is sampled as an extension-field element at offset 2*D_EF + folding_pow_offset.

blocks.heading: Publish alpha and final eq_partial on intra-WHIR buses
blocks.explain: WhirAlphaBus is a lookup bus: every alpha is registered by SumcheckAir (multiplicity alpha_lookup_count) and consumed by WhirFoldingAir and FinalPolyQueryEvalAir. WhirEqAlphaUBus is a permutation bus of cardinality one per proof: the last sumcheck row sends the full product, and FinalPolyMleEvalAir is the unique receiver. whir_opening_point_bus.receive pulls u[sumcheck_idx] — the u_cube coordinate for this sub-round — from the Stacking-published permutation bus.

blocks.html: The sumcheck group is closed. Back in WhirRoundAir, the round next dispatches num_queries queries. WhirQueryAir receives each batch.

blocks.heading: WhirQueryAir (query generation and per-query claim accumulation)
blocks.html: One row per (proof, round, query). It samples a transcript bit-vector sample, derives zi_root = omega^{sample} via ExpBitsLenBus, dispatches Merkle verification on VerifyQueryBus, and folds each per-query yi into the round's running claim with a gamma-Horner step.

blocks.heading: WhirQueryCols
blocks.code:
#[repr(C)]
#[derive(AlignedBorrow)]
pub struct WhirQueryCols<T> {
    pub is_enabled: T,
    // loop counters
    pub proof_idx: T,
    pub whir_round: T,
    pub query_idx: T,
    // first flags
    pub is_first_in_proof: T,
    pub is_first_in_round: T,
    pub tidx: T,
    pub num_queries: T,
    pub omega: T,
    pub sample: T,
    pub zi_root: T,
    pub zi: T,
    pub yi: [T; D_EF],
    pub gamma: [T; D_EF],
    pub gamma_pow: [T; D_EF],
    pub pre_claim: [T; D_EF],
    pub post_claim: [T; D_EF],
}
blocks.explain: Each row carries the sampled sample (interpreted as a bit-vector of length initial_log_domain_size - k - whir_round), the resulting zi_root, the domain point zi, the opened+folded value yi, and Horner state pre_claim / post_claim with gamma_pow. num_queries, omega, and gamma are received from WhirRoundAir.

blocks.heading: Receive per-round dispatch and seed gamma^2
blocks.explain: verify_queries_bus.receive at is_first_in_round discharges the dispatch from WhirRoundAir. At the same moment, gamma_pow is seeded to gamma^2 — matching WhirRoundAir's pre_claim = post_sumcheck_claim + gamma * y0, so the first query contributes with weight gamma^2 (y0 already got gamma^1). The penultimate constraint forces the last row of each round's block to have query_idx + 1 = num_queries.

blocks.heading: Intra-round chaining and final claim accumulation
blocks.explain: This is the per-query Horner accumulation for the WHIR claim. On every intra-round transition, gamma_pow multiplies by gamma, tidx increments by 1 (one bit sampled per query), pre_claim += gamma_pow * yi. On the last row of each round's block, pre_claim + yi * gamma_pow == post_claim, which matches what WhirRoundAir sent as post_claim = next_claim. This is the discharge of the “∑q yq·γq+2” term in the final acc.

blocks.heading: Sample query, publish query_idx on WhirQueryBus, dispatch VerifyQueryBus
blocks.explain: Three bus actions: (i) sample the query index as a field element on the transcript; (ii) publish (whir_round, query_idx+1, zi) on WhirQueryBus (where FinalPolyQueryEvalAir will receive it — note the +1 offset since query 0 was reserved for z0); (iii) dispatch Merkle verification on VerifyQueryBus, passing the raw sample as merkle_idx_bit_src and the already-proven (zi_root, zi, yi). Finally, ExpBitsLenBus constrains omega^{sample[0..initial_log_domain_size - k - whir_round]} == zi_root, i.e. zi_root is the correct domain point for the sampled index, with the bit-length decreasing by 1 per round (domain halving).

blocks.html: VerifyQueryBus dispatches to one of two AIRs depending on whether the round is initial (whir_round = 0) or non-initial. Let's do the initial case first.

blocks.heading: InitialOpenedValuesAir (round 0: open stacking commits + compute codeword)
blocks.html: This AIR runs only for the very first WHIR round. It opens rows of every stacking commitment at the sampled query index, Merkle-verifies them, and computes the round-0 codeword value codeword_vals[j] = ∑c μc · opened_rows[j][c] where c indexes columns across all stacking commits. It is the widest AIR in the module with a 5-level nested loop.

blocks.heading: InitialOpenedValuesCols
blocks.code:
// (proof_idx, query_idx, coset_idx, commit_idx, col_chunk_idx)
#[repr(C)]
#[derive(AlignedBorrow)]
pub(in crate::whir::initial_opened_values) struct InitialOpenedValuesCols<T> {
    pub proof_idx: T,
    pub query_idx: T,
    pub commit_idx: T,
    pub coset_idx: T,
    pub col_chunk_idx: T,
    pub is_first_in_proof: T,
    pub is_first_in_query: T,
    pub is_first_in_commit: T,
    pub is_first_in_coset: T,
    pub flags: [T; CHUNK],
    pub codeword_value_acc: [T; 4],
    pub codeword_value_next_acc: [T; 4],
    /// Stores clamped even powers for the current chunk base exponent `b`:
    /// mu^(min(b + 2k, opened_row_len - 1)) for k in [0, CHUNK / 2).
    pub mu_pows_even_clamped: [[T; 4]; CHUNK / 2],
    /// Stores mu^(min(b + CHUNK - 1, opened_row_len - 1)) for the current
    /// chunk base exponent `b`.
    /// Here opened_row_len is the opened row length for the current commit.
    pub mu_pow_last_clamped: [T; 4],
    pub mu: [T; 4],
    pub pre_state: [T; POSEIDON2_WIDTH],
    pub post_state: [T; POSEIDON2_WIDTH],
    pub twiddle: T,
    pub zi_root: T,
    pub zi: T,
    pub yi: [T; D_EF],
    pub merkle_idx_bit_src: T,
}
blocks.explain: The row key is the 5-tuple (proof, query, coset, commit, col_chunk). Within each col_chunk, flags[i] masks valid columns (since the last chunk of a commit may be short). mu_pows_even_clamped maintains μclamped exponent so that invalid columns contribute with the highest valid power (by clamping to opened_row_len - 1). pre_state / post_state are the Poseidon2 sponge states used to hash the leaf; they are chained across col_chunk rows within a commit.

blocks.heading: 5-level nested loop over (proof, query, coset, commit, col_chunk)
blocks.explain: The 5 levels enforce contiguous rows for (proof, query, coset, commit, col_chunk). flags[i] is a monotone mask: once it becomes 0 within a row, all subsequent flags[j > i] are forced to 0 (flags[i+1] = 1 => flags[i] = 1). This prefix structure is how the AIR handles a final partial chunk without branching.

blocks.heading: Receive mu; enforce coset rollover; recurrence for clamped mu powers
blocks.explain: mu is received on WhirMuBus (the permutation bus that Stacking feeds) and copied across all rows of a proof. mu_pows_even_clamped[k] maintains μmin(b+2k, opened_row_len-1): the clamping to the final valid power is what makes invalid columns contribute zero without branching on column-count. Note also the query-end assertion coset_idx = 2k-1: the last row of each query must exhaust all cosets.

blocks.heading: Codeword accumulation and sponge absorption of opened columns
blocks.explain: For every valid column c = col_chunk_idx * CHUNK + i, codeword_value_acc += mu_pow[c] * pre_state[i] where pre_state[i] is the opened value. The StackingIndicesBus lookup binds (commit_idx, col_idx) to exactly the columns Stacking authorized — if the prover tried to open an unregistered column, the lookup fails. The second capacity half of the sponge (pre_state[CHUNK..]) is chained across col_chunk rows within a commit.

blocks.heading: Discharge VerifyQueryBus, hash the leaf, and send MerkleVerifyBus
blocks.explain: On is_first_in_query we discharge the VerifyQueryBus dispatch from WhirQueryAir with whir_round = 0. Every row also triggers a Poseidon2 permutation on the pre_state -> post_state sponge step. At the last row of each commit, we send on MerkleVerifyBus with commit_minor = commit_idx and total_depth = initial_log_domain_size + 1 — the verifier will walk this merkle_idx_bit_src up to the root. This is how the opened-and-sponge-hashed leaf is tied to Stacking's committed data.

blocks.heading: Dispatch the codeword to WhirFoldingAir (one per coset)
blocks.explain: twiddle walks 1, ωk, ωk2, …, ωk2k-1 across cosets within one query. On the last row of each coset we send a WhirFoldingBus message with height = 0 (leaf of the folding tree), coset_size = 2k, and the accumulated codeword value. WhirFoldingAir will receive these leaves.

blocks.heading: NonInitialOpenedValuesAir (rounds 1+: open from codeword commitments)
blocks.html: For rounds ≥1 the codeword is a single extension-field value per coset, so the AIR is much simpler: a 4-level loop over (proof, round, query, coset), one Poseidon2 compression per coset, and a MerkleVerifyBus send. It binds to the codeword commitment registered on CommitmentsBus by WhirRoundAir.

blocks.heading: NonInitialOpenedValuesCols
blocks.code:
#[repr(C)]
#[derive(AlignedBorrow)]
pub(in crate::whir::non_initial_opened_values) struct NonInitialOpenedValuesCols<T> {
    pub is_enabled: T,
    // Indices
    pub proof_idx: T,
    pub whir_round: T,
    pub query_idx: T,
    pub coset_idx: T,
    // Flags
    pub is_first_in_proof: T,
    pub is_first_in_round: T,
    pub is_first_in_query: T,
    pub merkle_idx_bit_src: T,
    pub zi_root: T,
    pub zi: T,
    pub twiddle: T,
    pub value: [T; D_EF],
    pub value_hash: [T; CHUNK],
    pub yi: [T; D_EF],
}
blocks.explain: One row per (proof, round, query, coset). value is the opened codeword value (extension field), value_hash its Poseidon2 compression used as the Merkle leaf, twiddle the same domain coset twiddle as in the initial AIR.

blocks.heading: 4-level nested loop and VerifyQueryBus receive
blocks.explain: Counter uses whir_round - 1 (round 0 is handled by InitialOpenedValuesAir). The last row of each query has coset_idx = 2k-1, forcing the full set of cosets. The VerifyQueryBus dispatch from WhirQueryAir is discharged on is_first_in_query.

blocks.heading: Send folding leaf, hash value, MerkleVerifyBus with round-dependent depth
blocks.explain: Each row sends one folding leaf (every coset) and one Merkle-verify request. total_depth = initial_log_domain_size + 1 - whir_round implements the domain halving between rounds: the next-round codeword tree is one level shallower. commit_major = whir_round, commit_minor = 0 exactly matches the (major_idx = round+1, minor_idx = 0) key published by WhirRoundAir (since this AIR's whir_round is one-based relative to CommitmentsBus.major_idx).

blocks.heading: WhirFoldingAir (binary k-fold of opened values)
blocks.html: This AIR implements the binary folding tree that reduces coset_size = 2k leaves to a single value yi. It is purely algebraic: each row takes a pair (left_value, right_value) and produces value = left + (α - x)(left - right)/(2x). The root row (is_root = 1) checks value = y_final.

blocks.heading: WhirFoldingCols
blocks.code:
#[repr(C)]
#[derive(AlignedBorrow)]
pub struct WhirFoldingCols<T> {
    pub is_valid: T,
    pub proof_idx: T,
    pub whir_round: T,
    pub query_idx: T,
    pub is_root: T,
    pub coset_shift: T,
    pub coset_idx: T,
    /// Distance from the leaf layer in the folding tree.
    pub height: T,
    pub twiddle: T,
    pub coset_size: T,
    pub z_final: T,
    pub value: [T; 4],
    pub left_value: [T; 4],
    pub right_value: [T; 4],
    pub y_final: [T; 4],
    pub alpha: [T; 4],
}
blocks.explain: There is one row per internal node of each query's folding tree, plus the root. height is distance from the leaf layer (0 at leaves would not appear; leaves come from InitialOpenedValuesAir/NonInitialOpenedValuesAir); height = k at the root. alpha is the WHIR sumcheck challenge for this sub-round.

blocks.heading: Root boundary + fold formula
blocks.explain: The root enforces height = k, twiddle = 1, coset_idx = 0, z_final = coset_shift2, and value = y_final. The fold formula is value = left + (α - x)(left - right)/(2x) at x = twiddle * coset_shift, written in product-form (to avoid division) as (value - left)(2x) = (α - x)(left - right). This is the WHIR binary fold derived from Lagrange interpolation on {x, -x}.

blocks.heading: Children receives and parent send on WhirFoldingBus
blocks.code:
self.alpha_bus.lookup_key(
            builder,
            local.proof_idx,
            WhirAlphaMessage {
                idx: local.whir_round * AB::Expr::from_usize(self.k) + local.height - AB::Expr::ONE,
                challenge: local.alpha.map(Into::into),
            },
            local.is_valid,
        );
        self.folding_bus.receive(
            builder,
            local.proof_idx,
            WhirFoldingBusMessage {
                whir_round: local.whir_round.into(),
                query_idx: local.query_idx.into(),
                height: local.height - AB::Expr::ONE,
                coset_shift: local.coset_shift.into(),
                coset_size: AB::Expr::TWO * local.coset_size,
                coset_idx: local.coset_idx.into(),
                twiddle: local.twiddle.into(),
                value: local.left_value.map(Into::into),
                z_final: local.z_final.into(),
                y_final: local.y_final.map(Into::into),
            },
            local.is_valid,
        );
        self.folding_bus.receive(
            builder,
            local.proof_idx,
            WhirFoldingBusMessage {
                whir_round: local.whir_round.into(),
                query_idx: local.query_idx.into(),
                height: local.height - AB::Expr::ONE,
                coset_shift: local.coset_shift.into(),
                coset_size: AB::Expr::TWO * local.coset_size,
                coset_idx: local.coset_idx + local.coset_size,
                twiddle: -local.twiddle.into(),
                value: local.right_value.map(Into::into),
                z_final: local.z_final.into(),
                y_final: local.y_final.map(Into::into),
            },
            local.is_valid,
        );
        self.folding_bus.send(
            builder,
            local.proof_idx,
            WhirFoldingBusMessage {
                whir_round: local.whir_round.into(),
                query_idx: local.query_idx.into(),
                height: local.height.into(),
                coset_shift: local.coset_shift * local.coset_shift,
                coset_size: local.coset_size.into(),
                coset_idx: local.coset_idx.into(),
                twiddle: local.twiddle * local.twiddle,
                value: local.value.map(Into::into),
                z_final: local.z_final.into(),
                y_final: local.y_final.map(Into::into),
            },
            local.is_valid - local.is_root,
        );
blocks.explain: alpha is looked up on WhirAlphaBus with idx = whir_round * k + (height - 1) — matching SumcheckAir's publishing scheme so we pick the right sumcheck sub-round challenge for this folding level. The AIR then receives two child leaves at (height-1, coset_size*2) (the left with twiddle, right with coset_idx + coset_size and twiddle -> -twiddle), and sends the parent with squared coset_shift and twiddle. The send is gated by is_valid - is_root: the root does not send (it is consumed by is_root-branch equality value = y_final).

blocks.html:
At the root, the per-query value yi = y_final is pinned. This yi is exactly what WhirQueryAir carries in its columns and Horner-folds into the round claim (pre_claim += gamma_pow * yi).

Finally the phase closes with two AIRs that assemble the “accumulator equals claim” check.

blocks.heading: FinalPolyMleEvalAir (suffix = eval_mle(final_poly, u[t..]))
blocks.html: On the final WHIR round, the prover transmits 2log_final_poly_len extension-field coefficients of the final polynomial. This AIR observes them, treats them as leaves of a binary MLE-evaluation tree, and folds up the tree using the opening-point coordinates u[t..] where t = k * (num_whir_rounds - 1). The root value is multiplied by eq_partial (the prefix from SumcheckAir) to form the prefix·suffix contribution that must match the round-accumulated claim.

blocks.heading: FinalyPolyMleEvalCols
blocks.code:
#[repr(C)]
#[derive(AlignedBorrow)]
pub struct FinalyPolyMleEvalCols<T> {
    pub is_enabled: T,
    pub proof_idx: T,
    pub is_root: T,
    pub layer: T,
    pub layer_inv: T,
    pub node_idx: T,
    pub node_idx_inv: T,
    pub tidx_final_poly_start: T,
    pub is_nonleaf_and_first_in_layer: T,
    pub point: [T; D_EF],
    pub left_value: [T; D_EF],
    pub right_value: [T; D_EF],
    pub value: [T; D_EF],
    pub result: [T; D_EF],
    pub eq_alpha_u: [T; D_EF],
    pub num_nodes_in_layer: T,
}
blocks.explain: The trace has 2log_final_poly_len+1 - 1 non-skipped nodes per proof: 2log_final_poly_len leaves (layer 0), plus 2log_final_poly_len - 1 non-leaves. layer is the MLE layer index; node_idx is the node index within the layer; point is u[var_idx] for this layer; value is the folded MLE value at this node; the root's value is copied to result.

blocks.heading: Root / leaf identification; var_idx encoding
blocks.explain: is_nonleaf = layer_inv * layer is 1 for layer > 0 (i.e. any non-leaf node). The root has layer = num_vars = log_final_poly_len, node_idx = 0, num_nodes_in_layer = 1. var_idx = num_sumcheck_rounds + num_vars - layer maps the tree layer to the absolute variable index on WhirOpeningPointBus, letting this AIR pull the correct coordinate of u_cube for its layer.

blocks.heading: Receive opening point (permutation) and broadcast it (lookup)
blocks.explain: A subtle bus pattern: WhirOpeningPointBus (permutation) delivers u[var_idx] exactly once, to the first non-leaf node in each layer. That node re-registers the point on a lookup bus with multiplicity num_nodes_in_layer - 1, and every other non-leaf node in the same layer looks it up. This is cheaper than receiving a permutation message per node.

blocks.heading: Binary tree folding via FinalPolyFoldingBus
blocks.explain: Non-leaf nodes receive two child contributions on FinalPolyFoldingBus and send their own (except the root, which does not). The evaluation-form MLE fold is value = left + (right - left) * point, algebraically identical to (1 - point) * left + point * right. At the root, value = result.

blocks.heading: Leaves observe final_poly coefficients on transcript; register them for query AIR
blocks.explain: At each leaf, the value (which is just the raw coefficient final_poly[node_idx]) is observed on TranscriptBus at tidx_final_poly_start + node_idx * D_EF. It is also registered total_whir_queries times on the lookup bus WhirFinalPolyBus where FinalPolyQueryEvalAir will pull it per query. At the root, final_poly_mle_eval_bus fires with value = root_value * eq_alpha_u — this is exactly the “prefix · suffix” expression the verifier needs; the message is received by WhirRoundAir on the final round (where it sent local.final_poly_mle_eval).

blocks.heading: FinalPolyQueryEvalAir (query evaluations + final claim check)
blocks.html: This AIR runs one row per (proof, round, query, phase, eval_idx). For each round and query, it does two Horner-like phases: an eq phase that builds ∏j>t eq_1(alpha[j], query_pow[j]), and a final_poly phase (only on the last round) that Horner-evaluates final_poly at query_pow[last]. It accumulates everything into final_value_acc, which the last row sends on FinalPolyQueryEvalBus — closing the loop with WhirRoundAir.

blocks.heading: FinalPolyQueryEvalCols
blocks.code:
#[repr(C)]
#[derive(AlignedBorrow)]
pub(in crate::whir::final_poly_query_eval) struct FinalPolyQueryEvalCols<T> {
    pub is_enabled: T,
    // loop indices
    pub proof_idx: T,
    pub whir_round: T,
    pub query_idx: T,
    pub phase_idx: T,
    pub eval_idx: T,
    // is_first flags
    pub is_first_in_proof: T,
    pub is_first_in_round: T,
    pub is_first_in_query: T,
    pub is_first_in_phase: T,
    pub is_last_round: T,
    pub is_query_zero: T,
    pub query_pow: [T; D_EF],
    pub alpha: [T; D_EF],
    pub gamma: [T; D_EF],
    pub gamma_pow: [T; D_EF],
    pub final_poly_coeff: [T; D_EF],
    pub final_value_acc: [T; D_EF],
    pub gamma_eq_acc: [T; D_EF],
    pub horner_acc: [T; D_EF],
    pub do_carry: T,
}
blocks.explain: phase_idx = 0 is the eq-phase, phase_idx = 1 is the final_poly Horner phase. eval_idx counts within the phase. query_pow starts at the query point zi (received from WhirQueryBus) and is squared each eq-phase step — matching SumcheckAir's square-and-multiply MLE structure. final_value_acc is the running sum across queries; do_carry gates when a finished per-query contribution flows into it.

blocks.heading: Phase end constraints (5-level nested loop input)
blocks.explain: Each query in round r has an eq-phase of length k_whir * (num_whir_rounds - 1 - r) (0 on the last round) and a final_poly phase of length 2log_final_poly_len - 1 (on the last round only, because do_carry masks intermediate rounds). The last-row-of-phase constraints ensure full phases aren't cut short.

blocks.heading: query_pow chaining and Horner for final_poly
blocks.explain: In the eq-phase, query_pow is squared every step (so query_pow at eq-phase step j is zi2j). In the final_poly phase it is held constant (we are just Horner-evaluating final_poly at this fixed power). post_horner_acc = horner_acc * query_pow + final_poly_coeff is the standard Horner step.

blocks.heading: gamma_eq_acc and eq-phase product
blocks.explain: gamma_eq_acc starts at gamma_pow (which itself starts at gamma on the first row of a round and multiplies by gamma on every new query). In the eq-phase it multiplies by eq_1(alpha, query_pow) per step, so at the end of the eq-phase gamma_eq_acc = gamma^{q+1} * ∏j eq_1(alpha[j], zi2j).

blocks.heading: Per-query carry into final_value_acc
blocks.explain: do_carry fires at the end of a query (is_same_proof - is_same_query), except on the very first query of the last round (which is z0 and is accounted for by the caller's gamma * y0 term in WhirRoundAir). When do_carry fires, the per-query contribution gamma_eq_acc * post_horner_acc is added to final_value_acc. horner_acc is pinned to 0 in the eq-phase — the eq-phase only builds gamma_eq_acc, not the Horner sum.

blocks.heading: Pull query, alpha, gamma, final_poly_coeff from their buses
blocks.explain: Four bus pulls close the loop: WhirQueryBus seeds query_pow = zi (matching the dispatch from WhirQueryAir, including query_idx = 0 = z0 from WhirRoundAir); WhirAlphaBus provides the sumcheck challenge at alpha_idx = round_base + eval_idx; WhirGammaBus (permutation) receives gamma once per round; WhirFinalPolyBus pulls each final_poly coefficient registered by FinalPolyMleEvalAir. Finally, on the last row of the proof, FinalPolyQueryEvalBus.receive fires with value = final_value_acc + gamma_eq_acc * post_horner_acc, discharging the dispatch that WhirRoundAir sent on the last round (next_claim - final_poly_mle_eval).

blocks.html:
The final equality. WhirRoundAir's last-round send was FinalPolyQueryEvalMessage { value: next_claim - final_poly_mle_eval }. FinalPolyQueryEvalAir receives this with value = final_value_acc + gamma_eq_acc * post_horner_acc. Meanwhile, FinalPolyMleEvalAir forces final_poly_mle_eval = root_value * eq_alpha_u. Together these imply

next_claim == final_poly_mle_eval + final_value = prefix * suffix + ∑rounds,queries (gamma-weighted y + final-poly eval).

This is the M-WHIR final check. Any discrepancy between the folding sumcheck output and the polynomial opening causes a bus mismatch here, which is the last line of defense in the recursive verifier.

Having closed the claim, the recursion sub-circuit has discharged every ingredient of verify(vk_child, Extract(W)). With all eight WHIR AIRs satisfied, all their buses balanced, and the transcript pinned end-to-end, the outer STARK commits to a trace that could only exist if the child proof was valid.
